etimesThe mind that never logs out: How being always online hijacks our lives, an expert explainsOur always-on digital lives are straining mental health, turning our minds into perpetual night-shift workers. Constant connectivity fuels overthinking in relationships and work, blurring boundaries and demanding constant digital proof of care. This relentless stimulation prevents rest, leading to anxiety, fatigue, and sleep deprivation. Finding balance through digital boundaries and offline activities is crucial for well-being.

etimesHow to buy cheap flight tickets: Unlock hidden deals without breaking the bankAirfares are rising, but smart booking can save you money. Watch prices using alerts, book months in advance, and be flexible with dates. Consider nearby airports and search from different locations. Utilize loyalty points during off-peak times and follow fare mistake accounts for incredible deals. Splitting journeys can also offer savings.
